channel.title: FrontionNews - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Bulgaria's Radev Claims 'Uncontested Victory' as Progressive Coalition Sweeps Elections
item.description: Former President Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria coalition claimed victory with 37-38% of the vote, sweeping aside the center-right GERB party that dominated politics for the last decade. The victory comes after mass protests brought down the government in December.

item.title: Canada's Carney Says Economic Ties With US Have Become a Weakness
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said the country's strong economic ties to the United States were once a strength but are now a weakness that must be corrected, citing Trump's tariffs that have affected auto and steel workers.

item.title: Israeli Ministers Celebrate Re-establishment of West Bank Settlement Two Decades After Eviction
item.description: Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich and other officials attended a ceremony reopening the Sa-Nur settlement in the occupied West Bank, 21 years after it was evacuated. The move is part of Israel's reversal of its 2005 disengagement policy.

item.title: US and Iran Accuse Each Other of Ceasefire Violations as Hormuz Standoff Continues
item.description: President Trump accused Iran of firing on French and British ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran's foreign ministry called the US blockade a 'war crime.' Indian-flagged vessels were among those attacked, prompting India to summon Iran's ambassador.

item.title: Allies Fear Rushed US-Iran Framework Deal Could Backfire and Entrench Problems
item.description: Diplomats worry that a headline-grabbing framework agreement between Washington and Tehran could entrench deeper problems, leaving technical issues unresolved. The concerns come as US negotiators prepare for talks in Islamabad.

item.title: Iran Denies Agreeing to New Talks as Trump Claims Negotiations Continue
item.description: Iran denied it had agreed to new nuclear talks, contradicting Trump's claim that negotiations would continue. The denial came amid escalating tensions over the Strait of Hormuz and a US naval blockade.

item.title: Merz and Lula Stress European-Brazilian Cooperation Amid Global Uncertainty
item.description: German Chancellor Merz and Brazilian President Lula emphasized close cooperation between Europe and Brazil, discussing trade, climate policy, and multilateral institutions amid shifting global alliances.

item.title: Eight Children Killed in Louisiana Mass Shooting, Police Say
item.description: Eight children between the ages of 1 and 14 were killed in a mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana. Police are investigating the incident as one of the deadliest attacks on children in recent U.S. history.

item.title: Ukraine Pushes for Europe to Build Defense System Against Ballistic Weapons
item.description: Ukraine is urging European nations to develop a joint missile defense system to counter ballistic threats, as Russia intensifies attacks on Ukrainian cities and infrastructure.

item.title: Israeli Military Publishes Map of South Lebanon Territory Under Its Control
item.description: The Israeli military published for the first time a map of its new deployment line inside Lebanon, bringing dozens of mostly abandoned Lebanese villages under its control just days after a ceasefire with Hezbollah took effect. The move signals Israel's intention to maintain a security buffer zone despite the truce.

item.title: Pope Leo Urges Angola to Heal Divisions at Mass Drawing 130,000
item.description: Pope Leo urged Angolans to overcome divisions after decades of war, first at a Mass in a field outside Luanda and then in a prayer at a site that was once a hub for transatlantic slavery. The events jointly drew roughly 130,000 people as the Pope continued his Africa tour.

item.title: Bulgaria's Pro-Russian Former President Takes Strong Lead in Election
item.description: The party of Bulgaria's pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev is set to win Sunday's parliamentary vote with 37.5% of the votes according to exit polls. Radev, who opposes sanctions against Russia and military aid for Ukraine, campaigned to stamp out corruption and end a spiral of weak governments.

item.title: Trump Says US Navy Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Defying Hormuz Blockade
item.description: President Trump announced the U.S. Navy forcibly seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship, the Touska, that tried to bypass the naval blockade near the Strait of Hormuz. A Navy destroyer "blew a hole in the engineroom" after the vessel refused to stop.

item.title: North Korea Fires Short-Range Ballistic Missiles as UN Warns of Nuclear Advances
item.description: North Korea launched multiple short-range ballistic missiles from its Sinpo area, with projectiles flying about 140 kilometers toward eastern waters. The test came days after the UN nuclear watchdog warned Pyongyang is making 'very serious' advances in its nuclear weapons program, marking the seventh missile test this year.

item.title: Asian Development Bank Chief Warns of Yen Pressure From Japan's Slow Rate Hikes
item.description: ADB President Masato Kanda warned that the Japanese yen could face further pressure if markets perceive the Bank of Japan as moving too slowly on addressing inflationary risks. The comments highlight ongoing currency tensions as global central banks navigate post-war economic uncertainty.

item.title: Pope Leo Calls for 'Weapons to Fall Silent' as Ukraine War Intensifies
item.description: Pope Leo decried the intensification of the war in Ukraine, calling 'for the weapons to fall silent and for the path of dialogue to be followed.' The Pope's appeal comes amid escalating Russian drone attacks and Ukrainian strikes on Russian military infrastructure.

item.title: Russian Strikes Kill Two in Ukraine as Kyiv Hits Russian Drone Factory
item.description: Russian drone attacks killed a 16-year-old boy in Chernihiv and a man in Kherson, while Ukraine's navy struck the Atlant Aero drone factory in Taganrog using Neptune cruise missiles. The facility develops strike and reconnaissance drones and components for UAVs carrying guided bombs.

item.title: Belarus's Lukashenko Says Ready to Meet Trump Once 'Big Deal' Is Prepared
item.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko said he would be ready to meet US President Donald Trump once a 'big deal' between the two countries has been prepared. The statement signals potential for Belarus-West diplomacy as the country remains a key Russian ally in the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: IMF and World Bank Meetings Show Limits of Crisis Response Without US Leadership
item.description: Spring meetings of the IMF and World Bank revealed the limits of global institutions in mitigating shocks from the Iran war and other crises, showing that counting on US leadership to resolve global problems is no longer the guarantee it once was. The meetings highlighted a shift in the global financial order.

item.title: Spain and Mexico End Diplomatic Spat Over Colonial Past With Handshake
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum laid to rest a years-long diplomatic dispute with Spain over the Spanish colonial past during her visit to Barcelona. The reconciliation follows King Felipe VI's March acknowledgment of abuses during the conquest of the Americas, ending tensions that began in 2019.

item.title: EU Leaders Back Major New Defense Spending Plans at Ukraine Summit
item.description: European Union leaders committed to a massive step in defense cooperation following decades of hesitation, spurred by the Ukraine conflict and uncertainty about US commitment to NATO. The summit marks a significant shift toward European defense autonomy.

item.title: US Backs South Africa Rare Earths Project to Counter China's Mineral Dominance
item.description: The Trump administration is supporting a $50 million project to extract rare earth elements from mining waste in South Africa, part of accelerated US efforts to reduce reliance on China for minerals crucial to electronics, defense systems, and electric vehicles. The project continues despite a major diplomatic rift between Washington and Pretoria.

item.title: Pope Leo Downplays Feud With Trump, Says 'Not in My Interest' to Debate
item.description: Pope Leo XIV sought to downplay his feud with US President Donald Trump, saying reporting about his comments during his Africa tour 'has not been accurate in all its aspects.' The Pope said it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate Trump but will continue preaching peace.

item.title: UK Seeks EU Reset as Iran War Strains Ties With Trump's America
item.description: Britain's government is set to announce legislation next month to move the country closer to the European Union, as the Iran war and Trump's unpredictability sour the UK's 'special relationship' with the United States. Prime Minister Keir Starmer's EU 'reset' bill would give ministers powers to align UK standards with EU single market rules.

item.title: Mediation Fails in Dispute Over Franco-German Fighter Jet Project
item.description: Mediators have failed to resolve a dispute between companies involved in the Franco-German FCAS fighter jet project, Handelsblatt reported. The setback threatens the Future Combat Air System program, a flagship European defense collaboration aimed at developing next-generation fighter aircraft.
